item.title: Ukrainian porn star helps soldiers injured during the war with calendar shoot to raise money for rehabilitation and prosthetic costs
item.description: Ukrainian adult film actress Yulia Seniuk, known professionally as Josephine Jackson, organised a charity calendar photoshoot featuring wounded soldiers who fought on the frontlines. The proceeds from the calendar sales will be used to help other injured soldiers with rehabilitation and prosthetic costs, according to the Ukrainian news outlet Obozrevatel. The photoshoot, which took place in a luxurious Ukrainian theatre, featured Yulia Seniuk posing with the soldiers in elegant evening gowns. The 12 soldiers, chosen to represent each month of the year, wore suits, ties, or tuxedos. All of the participating veterans have lost limbs in service, and the shoot did not attempt to hide their prosthetics or wheelchairs, as per the news outlet. https://www.instagram.com/reel/C4h25D6IFi0/?utm_source=ig_embed Yulia Seniuk explained that the photoshoot's intention was to showcase the soldiers in a dignified manner, different from how they are typically portrayed - either in uniform or recovering in medical facilities. The actress said that the opulent venue and refined attire were carefully selected to present the soldiers in a dignified manner, contrasting with the typical depictions of them either in uniform or as patients in rehabilitation centers. "This transformation reveals them from a completely different side, showing what refined, elegant, and handsome gentlemen they are. It gives them more self-confidence and a sense of self-sufficiency," she told Obozrevatel. "These are the emotions we should give them, not pity and tears." Yulia's Instagram video provides a glimpse behind the scenes of the photoshoot, revealing the soldiers laughing and having fun during the experience.
item.dc:creator: Herebychoice

item.title: Divers Witness A Pod Of Sperm Whales Rise To The Surface In An Intertwined Dance
item.description: During periods of socializing, Sperm Whales participate in various activities that foster affinity and strengthen social bonds. They communicate effusively and engage in kinesthetic forms of interaction, frequently rubbing against or nuzzling one another. Juveniles and calves enjoy rigorous bouts of play that involve jostling, bubbling, twirling, intertwining their bodies, and jawing each other with open mouths and mock ferocity. These behaviors are performed with the elegance of a choreographed dance. #the_more_you_know #awesome #animals #aww #mildly_interesting
item.dc:creator: OceansRust

item.title: Commander William Adama, Battlestar Galactica, has some words for you.
item.description: Clip: https://www.youtube.com/shorts/sz2QN8_VvoM Commander Adama says this line in the second episode of the first season, titled "Water The quote serves as a powerful warning against the potential dangers of blurring the lines between military and police functions. It suggests that when military power is directed inward and used against a nation's own citizens, it can lead to tyranny and the oppression of the population. This idea aligns with the concept of civilian control of the military, a fundamental principle in many democracies, including the United States. Civilian control ensures that military actions are subordinate to the political decisions of elected officials, safeguarding democratic values and preventing the military from becoming a threat to personal liberty. Historically, the separation of military and police powers has been a concern in the United States, as evidenced by the passage of the Posse Comitatus Act of 1878. This act generally prohibits the use of federal military forces for domestic law enforcement, reflecting a long-standing American tradition that views military interference in civilian affairs as dangerous.
item.dc:creator: psuedon

item.title: Just screaming into the void
item.description: I tried opening up to my friend group and was told to stop bumming everyone out. Taking the steps to get better anyway. I know this won't be seen, but I needed to say something, anything; to acknowledge my own efforts at least.

item.dc:creator: Gabriel971337

item.title: I'll try not to question it.
item.description: It was a pretty standard two hour interview. One hour in person, and one hour in an ad hoc lab environment (a desk, a couple of computers, equipment and a list of tasks). I felt pretty comfortable during the in-person interview and thought I handled it well, but then I was thrown a curveball in the lab portion. I met a router I'd never seen before, it's not your standard SoHo router. I had to fully configure it and then go through a list of easier tasks like creating a fileshare, setting up a network printer... truly easy things. I never got to those things because I spent the entire hour learning how to configure this router. The guy still gave me the job! (shrug) I'm going to have a hard time managing my imposter syndrome for the first few months but DAMN! Feels good to be employed again!
item.dc:creator: ThatSonOfaBitch

item.title: Aussie Journalist Finds out about American Press Freedoms!

item.title: I QUIT MANAGEMENT AND I FEEL SO MUCH BETTER.
item.description:
I sent this to a couple of the servers right after sending my farewell message in our work group chat (included later) My management style is just my shitposting self but IRL. I was professional when it came to not hanging out with the servers outside of work though..so I'm excited to finally be allowed to be friends.
Management, of course, has its ups and downs. Ups: 1.) Working with hilarious people who make fun of rude customers with you 2.) Example 2 3.) Example 3 Downs: 1.) Being the employee who has to deescalate any given problem regardless of how severe or petty it may be. 2.) Being the message relayer between the servers to the owner and vise versa. 3.) Always being the responsible one whenever anything goes wrong on your watch. 4.) Being too mentally and physically tired to do the one fucking thing I love do.
Being the manager can be such a negative feedback loop. I am always the first one summoned when a customer begins to become high maintenance. I've had to fetch a middle aged woman a fresh waffle because hers wasn't hot anymore after she let it sit there and get cold during her 40 minute chit chat with the lady who asked for all of her food to be on completely separate plates so they don't touch each other.
My husband I are both veterans, and we each have the privilege of receiving VA disability every month. After YEARS of struggling financially and never feeling like we were going to make ends meet, the VA came through, and now all of our bills are covered on the 1st of the month every month. Both of our payments combined covers our bills damn near to the penny though. So my husband and I both worked a couple of days a week just to have some extra money for groceries and gas. For the first time in our marriage, we had the opportunity to relax and figure out what we want to do with ourselves. Last October, he and I both took a leave from our jobs and streamed for 744 hours to raise money for Extra-Life 2024. We loved it. We were actually bummed when the stream finally came to an end. It was then that we realized that we did find what made us happy, and we wanted to commit to streaming full time. I also decided that we were going to attend Twitch Con in San Diago in October 2025..
..but I was going to need to save up. That's why the management position sounded so tempting when it was proposed to me. I told her I'd consider working as a temporary manager until October, but I did not want to make a career of management. I started the position in January. It's a breakfast restaurant, so I always had to wake up stupid early which meant I couldn't stay up late streaming. I became inconsistent with my streaming schedule, and each day it was becoming easier and easier to just go to bed instead of socializing with my friends in discord...and I was just fuckin sad. All the time.
Mondays and Tuesdays are our slowest days. It's quiet. It's boring. It's not stimulating st all. I'm not supposed to be on my phone, and I can only check up on the 5 tables we have until I get annoying. So, I think. Too much. It's tortuous. I get stuck in my head about how pissed off I am that I have to waste 20 hours of the time I could be spending streaming. I mean, the whole reason I took this job was to save money for Twitch Con. What's the fucking point? I wasn't even streaming.
I had an anxiety attack ON THE WAY to work last Tuesday. I couldn't stop crying...you know when your sinuses get all swollen so you can't breathe out your nose? So I wasn't getting enough air, so I started breathing through my mouth, then I was getting TOO MUCH air, and I started to feel light headed. My hands were tingling, but I had to clock in in 2 minutes. I got out of the car and kept my head down and walked straight to the broom closet sized office and shut the door behind me. The only light from from the glow of the customer monitor. I kept trying to breath, but then I began doubling down an my anxious thoughts when I caught a glimpse of my reflection and saw how puffy my eyes were, how red my nose was, all the splotchess up and down my neck, chest, and arms. It didn't matter if I could stop crying, it was still obvious that I had been. All I could think was "I wish I could leave and have this anxiety attack in the privacy of my own home" But I couldn't. I HAD to fake it...for the employees, for the customers, for the paycheck...
The paycheck I forgot I don't necessarily need. It's certainly not worth my happiness. That day was my breaking point. I called the owner and told her everything... and she was actually very empathetic and understanding. Today, I completed my last week of management after that call, and I feel....relieved. This was my farewell message:
I'm excited to be a fucking goofball with my friends again. I miss this dumb shit so much. I have so much fun.
Potato Bug tax
Bonus Potato Bug tax
Bonus bonus Deaf Cat Magoo (short for Oogala Magoogala) tax
So, now I'll actually have the time and energy to commit to streaming full time on twitch.tv/bottomshelfvodka We also post non stop memes and stupidity on discord.gg/bottomshelfvodka TL:DR Life if better
item.dc:creator: BottomShelfVodka
